free_text: Gamma rays resulting from interactions of 14.7 MeV
The scattering samples were all in the form of right
circular cylinders 3 in. long and 14 in. in diam. The
elemental samples except sodium and potassium were
machined to this configuration and the compound samples
were contained in a thin-walled Lucite cylinder of
4-in. long by 4 m diameter NaI(Tl) crystal
mounted on a 34 in. EMI9531S photomultiplier tube.
The effective neutron fluence at the sample position
was measured by activating a series of 1.5 in. in diam.
by 0.25-in. thick sulphur disks assembled as a 1.5 in.
in diam. by 3-in. long cylinder to conform with the
uncertainty in the area under the
